query: Audit the CSS in `docs/assets/site.css` for W3C design token architecture compliance. Identify which values are raw primitives, which should be semantic tokens, and which are component-level tokens.
expected_skill_contribution: The design-systems-arch skill applies the three-layer token architecture (Primitive → Semantic → Component) to classify existing CSS values.
baseline_gaps: Agent audits CSS quality without applying the three-layer token classification.
Agent does not distinguish semantic intent from primitive value — treats all tokens as equivalent.
Agent lists issues without proposing the corrected three-layer structure.
expected_behavior: Classifies every CSS custom property into one of three layers: Primitive (raw value), Semantic (intent-based alias), or Component (component-specific override).
Identifies values that bypass the semantic layer (component referencing primitives directly) as token architecture violations.
Proposes a three-layer token migration plan with the exact rename and alias chain for each violation.
States which violations break theming capability and why.

query: Design a multi-brand token architecture for the harness documentation site that supports a 'default' brand and a 'high-contrast' accessibility theme. Define the full three-layer token structure and the theming strategy.
expected_skill_contribution: The design-systems-arch skill applies the Multi-brand architecture pattern: semantic layer swap with primitives and components unchanged.
baseline_gaps: Agent proposes separate CSS files per theme rather than a semantic-layer-swap architecture.
Agent does not define the Primitive layer as brand-invariant — allows brand tokens at the wrong layer.
Agent does not explain how the high-contrast theme satisfies WCAG AA contrast requirements through token overrides.
expected_behavior: Defines the Primitive layer as brand-invariant (shared color values used by both brands).
Defines the Semantic layer as the brand-variable layer — swapping this layer alone changes the brand.
Shows how the high-contrast theme overrides only Semantic tokens, leaving Primitives and Component tokens unchanged.
Verifies that the high-contrast Semantic tokens satisfy WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast ratios for text.

query: Conduct a full design system maturity assessment for the harness UI (docs site + eval report). Map it to the six-level maturity model, identify the current level and the blockers to the next level, and propose a governance model.
expected_skill_contribution: The design-systems-arch skill applies the full maturity model (L0–L5), identifies level-specific blockers, and adds governance structure recommendations.
baseline_gaps: Agent assesses the design system quality without applying the six-level maturity model.
Agent does not identify the specific blockers preventing progression to the next maturity level.
Agent does not propose a governance model — only technical improvements.
expected_behavior: Maps the current state to a specific maturity level (L0–L5) with evidence from the audited files.
Identifies the specific blockers to the next level — not general improvements, but the exact criteria unmet.
Proposes a governance model: who owns the system, how exceptions are requested, how contributions are reviewed.
Estimates the effort to reach each subsequent maturity level as a rough LOE (days/weeks).
